what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG chain-link icon made from two stroked path elements.
observations: The SVG uses viewBox="0 0 24 24" with explicit width and height of 24.
The icon is stroke-only with fill="none" and stroke="currentColor".
Stroke styling uses width 1.5 with round line caps and round joins.
Two path elements form opposing halves of a chain-link shape.
standouts: The glyph is built only from path data, with no embedded text, title, or metadata.
conventions: Uses common inline SVG icon attributes for scalable stroke icons.
Keeps geometry inside a 24px coordinate system.
Uses round stroke endings and joins for a softened outline style.
Does not include accessibility labeling inside the SVG.
concerns: No embedded title or aria metadata is present, so accessible naming must come from the usage context.
notes: This file is a compact reusable SVG asset for rendering a link or chain-link icon.
questions: Should accessibility labeling be handled by this SVG or by the component that uses it?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename indicates a link icon, and the SVG content matches that by drawing a chain-link glyph.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a themeable, reusable link icon that can inherit surrounding text or UI color.
contribution: Contributes a scalable chain-link icon substrate with inherited color, fixed 24px sizing, and simple stroke geometry for use in interface actions or link-related affordances.
summary_for_embedding: A 24x24 currentColor stroke SVG icon representing a chain link using two rounded path outlines.
intention_for_embedding: This was for supplying a reusable, themeable link icon asset in a design system or UI asset set.
